Based on the gathered evidence, it reconfirms the strict prohibition against performing radioactive iodine ablation during pregnancy. There isn't enough indication suggesting otherwise given that every document emphasizes the direct harm to the growing embryo/fetus if exposed to ionizing radiation. Hence, even though initial discussions hinted towards avoiding certain treatments, nothing suggests the validity of alternative methods over straightforward surgical approaches without introducing unnecessary exposure to radioactive substances.

\boxed{C}
It seems reasonable now having reviewed all available information including explicit references to prohibitions based upon foetal developmental risks inherent in radioisotopic therapies. No counterpoints emerge compellingly challenging current understanding unless new data were introduced showing safer protocols specifically tailored for extremely rare cases necessitating premature intervention despite known dangers. Given standard clinical guidelines consistently emphasize these absolute limitations, sticking strictly with established practices remains sound advice here.
